mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling over recent months, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns turn positive. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating at the same time; a falling policy rate with stable CPIAUCSL should lift equities.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month declines in FEDFUNDS, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable and positive signal precision falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and slower demand, which lowers expected corporate sales and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The effect is most direct when PAYEMS is also decelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign remains negative even when PAYEMS is accelerating rather than slowing.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO indicates rising industrial output and better near-term earnings momentum for cyclical firms, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal should be strongest when output gains are broad enough to exceed prior-month noise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if above-average INDPRO readings are followed by negative or flat 60-day SPX returns in more than half of episodes, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO changes and forward SPX returns is persistently below zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS implies stronger consumer and business spending, which raises revenue growth expectations for large-cap equities and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be visible when retail sales growth is broad-based rather than a one-off spike.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if forward returns after strong sales growth are not statistically above returns after weak sales growth over any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound, measured by HOUST trending higher, signals improving durable-goods demand and credit transmission that supports equities with a lag. The mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because housing strength precedes broader consumption and investment gains.
failure_condition: Reject if housing rebounds are followed by non-positive 60-day SPX returns in most triggered cases, or if the signal loses predictive power when interest rates are not falling.

mechanism_narrative: A high federal funds rate compresses equity valuations when inflation is not falling fast enough to justify imminent easing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when FEDFUNDS is above a tightening threshold and CPIAUCSL remains elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not produce below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the negative effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL is falling.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising and UNRATE is flat or falling, labor income and employment breadth are improving together, which supports consumer spending and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The signal is a short-horizon confirmation of labor-market resilience.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals weakening final demand and deteriorating earnings expectations, which should push S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter. The claim is strongest when both broad output and spending weaken together, not when only one series softens.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns negative and RSAFS is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if combined weakness in GDPC1 and RSAFS is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms a single-series baseline in walk-forward testing.
